name: agentic-loop
description: Agentic software development loop: task intake → planning with reviewer feedback iterations (max 5) → human approval → coding agents (frontend / backend / database) → code review with reiteration → documentation summary

settings.action: Describe the task: feature/problem, specific requirements, acceptance criteria, and relevant context (tech stack, constraints).

Using the original task and any prior reviewer feedback, produce or update the implementation plan with sections: 1) Executive Summary, 2) Task Breakdown (subtasks with owners: frontend/backend/database), 3) Acceptance Criteria (specific, testable), 4) Technical Approach (per-component design decisions), 5) Dependencies and Risks, 6) Revision History (append a dated entry per review cycle noting changes). Output the full updated plan document.

Increment review_iteration by 1. Review the plan against the original task requirements for: completeness, technical feasibility, correctness of acceptance criteria, edge case coverage, and clarity of task breakdown. If fully satisfactory, set has_feedback=false. Otherwise set has_feedback=true and append specific, actionable feedback items to the plan's Revision History detailing exactly what must be improved.
Review the plan document above (including revision history). Reply 'approve' to proceed with implementation, or 'reject' with specific comments to revise the plan further.

conditions.condition: human requests changes to the plan

settings.action: Implement all frontend components per the approved plan: UI layouts, forms, client-side state management, API call integrations, and input validation. Meet every plan acceptance criterion. Produce complete, working code files. Document: what was built, which acceptance criteria were addressed, and any implementation decisions made.

Implement all backend components per the approved plan: API endpoints, business logic services, authentication/authorization, input validation, and external system integrations. Meet every plan acceptance criterion. Produce complete, working code files. Document: what was built, which acceptance criteria were addressed, and any implementation decisions made.

Implement all database components per the approved plan: schema definitions, migrations, indexes, queries, and the data access layer. Meet every plan acceptance criterion. Produce complete, working code files. Document: what was built, which acceptance criteria were addressed, and any implementation decisions made.

Increment code_review_iteration by 1. Review all frontend, backend, and database code against the plan's acceptance criteria for: correctness, completeness of each criterion, code quality and consistency, security issues, and component integration. If all acceptance criteria are met and code is production-ready, set code_approved=true. Otherwise set code_approved=false and produce a review report specifying which acceptance criteria are unmet and what each coding agent must fix.

Write an implementation summary document with sections: 1) What Was Built (feature overview and components delivered), 2) Architecture (frontend/backend/database integration), 3) Acceptance Criteria Checklist (each criterion and status: met/partially met/not met), 4) Setup and Deployment Instructions (step-by-step), 5) Code Review Summary (findings and known limitations), 6) Future Improvements (recommended follow-up work). Ground every statement in the approved plan and actual code produced by the coding agents.

variables.description: Indicates whether the plan reviewer has feedback requiring further revision; set to 'true' or 'false' by the plan reviewer agent.
Counter tracking how many plan review cycles have been completed; incremented by the plan reviewer agent each iteration.
The human's response to the plan approval step; 'reject' if the plan is rejected, 'changes_requested' if the human requests modifications, 'approve' if approved.
Indicates whether the code reviewer has approved all code as production-ready; set to 'true' or 'false' by the code reviewer agent.
Counter tracking how many code review cycles have been completed; incremented by the code reviewer agent each iteration.
